behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: three source collectors -> aggregation payload -> summary-generation caller. A unit test on only one function can miss wiring/field-drop bugs across modules. Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess is required (sources can be stubbed), no dependence on real model output quality, and no multi-step agent loop. Rejection gate: System is disqualified because behavior text targets "content passed" (in-process composition), not real network semantics; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because it does not require validating model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report pipeline in-process with deterministic stubs for metrics, issue tracker, and deployment log clients. Intercept the exact payload sent to summary-generation and assert it contains all three source sections/keys with correct mapped values.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears in cross-module error propagation and fallback rendering: source client error -> orchestrator continues -> report section annotated. Unit is too low because interaction between collector, orchestrator, and renderer is required. Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required (simulate unavailable source via stub exception/status), no real AI quality dependence, no multi-agent chain. Rejection gate: System is disqualified because behavior text is about graceful degradation logic, not real transport-layer behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute one full in-process report run with two source stubs returning data and one stub raising an unavailable error. Assert run does not abort, output contains a `data unavailable` marker only for the failed section, and other sections are still populated.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on what the summary generator actually writes (content fidelity), which is model-output quality and can vary run-to-run. Deterministic stubs would hide hallucination/omission failures. Rejection gate: Integration is disqualified by behavior text "accurately represents" (semantic output quality), not just routing/retry/payload formatting.
behaviors.test_description: Run summary generation against the real model using representative input fixtures with known key values. Evaluate whether required facts/figures are preserved and not contradicted (allowing wording variance), across repeated runs to catch non-deterministic misses.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real external contracts: data-source HTTP/API calls plus real email delivery/inbox behavior. In-process mocks can hide auth, serialization, SMTP/API delivery, or mailbox acceptance issues. Rejection gate: Agentic is considered but disqualified by behavior text focusing on transport outcome "reaches ... inbox correctly" rather than summary quality; Workflow is disqualified because this is one linear end-to-end execution, not multiple agentic decisions chained for correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run the scheduled script in an environment wired to real (test) internal endpoints and a real email test inbox. Verify report generation completes and the recipient inbox receives the message with expected subject/body structure and attachments/format.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Minimum real failure boundary is local persistence + retry orchestration across two runs: sender failure -> file fallback -> later run reloads and retries. This needs module/state handoff plus filesystem persistence, but not necessarily real network/email infra. Integration brake: can simulate unreachable email service via sender stub; no AI-output dependence; no multi-step agent pipeline. Rejection gate: System is disqualified because behavior text can be satisfied without real SMTP/network semantics; core risk is retry state management.
behaviors.test_description: Execute run #1 with email sender stubbed to fail unreachable; assert report file is written to local storage. Execute run #2 with sender stub recovering; assert persisted report is retried/sent and fallback file state is cleared or marked delivered.
